page.title: Cabopino vs Estepona Marina: Best Charter Departure

data.meta_description: Cabopino vs Estepona marina departure: which is best for your boat charter? Compare distances, facilities, and nearby hotspots to choose your perfect launch point.
data.summary: Compare Cabopino and Estepona marinas as departure points for your Marbella boat charter, weighing distance, facilities, and access to coastal hotspots. We break down the pros and cons to help you choose the best marina for your trip.
key_facts: Cabopino Marina is 10 km east of Marbella, Estepona Marina 20 km west.
Cabopino offers direct access to Cala del Faro and sand-dune beaches.
Estepona Marina is closer to Gibraltar and Sotogrande for longer cruises.
Both marinas have full facilities: fuel, parking, restaurants, and chandlers.
Charter boats from Puerto Banús can pick you up at either marina for a fee.
Estepona is typically less crowded than Puerto Banús, especially in peak season.
Cabopino has a quieter, more relaxed vibe with a small fishing-port feel.
Distance from Marbella centre: Cabopino 15 min drive, Estepona 25 min.

faq.q: Which marina is closer to Marbella town centre?
faq.a: Cabopino Marina is closer, roughly 10 km (15 minutes) east of Marbella centre. Estepona Marina is about 20 km (25 minutes) west. If you're staying on the Golden Mile or in central Marbella, Cabopino is a quicker drive.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat from Cabopino or Estepona Marina?
faq.a: Yes, our boats depart from Puerto Banús but we can arrange a pickup at either Cabopino or Estepona Marina for an additional fee. Contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability and pricing for your chosen vessel.

faq.q: What are the main attractions near Cabopino Marina?
faq.a: Cabopino is famous for its sand-dune beach (Dunas de Artola) and the nearby Cala del Faro, a secluded cove ideal for swimming. The marina itself has a handful of tapas bars and a relaxed, low-key atmosphere.

faq.q: What are the main attractions near Estepona Marina?
faq.a: Estepona Marina is close to the historic old town with its flower-filled streets, plus the beaches of Playa del Cristo and Playa de la Rada. It's also a short cruise from Gibraltar and the luxury port of Sotogrande.

faq.q: Which marina is less crowded in summer?
faq.a: Estepona Marina is generally less crowded than Puerto Banús, especially in July and August. Cabopino is also quieter but can get busy on weekends with local boaters. Both offer a more relaxed alternative to Banús.

faq.q: Which marina is better for a day trip to Gibraltar?
faq.a: Estepona Marina is the better choice for a Gibraltar day trip, as it's only 25 nautical miles from the Rock. Cabopino is about 45 NM east, making Estepona a more practical departure point for that route.

faq.q: Are there fuel stations at both marinas?
faq.a: Yes, both Cabopino and Estepona marinas have fuel docks with diesel and petrol. They are open daily, though hours may vary in low season. We recommend checking ahead if you plan to refuel before departure.

faq.q: Can I park my car at Cabopino or Estepona Marina?
faq.a: Both marinas have paid parking lots. Cabopino's parking can fill quickly on summer weekends, while Estepona offers more spaces. If you're chartering with us, we can advise on the best place to park for your pickup.

data.body_html:
Choosing between Cabopino and Estepona Marina for your boat charter departure in Marbella depends on where you're staying and what you want to explore. Cabopino, 10 km east of Marbella, offers quick access to Cala del Faro and quiet sand-dune beaches. Estepona, 20 km west, puts you closer to Gibraltar and Sotogrande for longer coastal cruises. Both are less crowded than Puerto Banús and provide full marina facilities. This guide compares distance, amenities, and nearby hotspots to help you decide which launch point suits your trip best. For a full overview of our fleet and departure options, visit our boat charter hub.

Distance and Travel Time from Marbella

Cabopino Marina is roughly 10 km east of Marbella town centre, a 15-minute drive via the A-7 motorway. Estepona Marina is 20 km west, about 25 minutes by car. If you're staying on the Golden Mile or in central Marbella, Cabopino is the quicker option. For visitors based near Estepona or Sotogrande, Estepona Marina is more convenient. Both marinas have ample taxi and ride-share options from Marbella centre.

Facilities and Amenities

Both marinas offer fuel docks, parking, restaurants, and chandlers. Cabopino has a smaller, fishing-port feel with a handful of tapas bars and a supermarket. Estepona Marina is larger, with more dining choices, a yacht club, and a chandlery. Wi-Fi is available at both, though Estepona's signal is generally stronger. If you need a full-service marina with a wider range of shops, Estepona edges ahead. For a quick bite and a relaxed vibe, Cabopino works well.

Access to Coastal Hotspots

Cabopino is the gateway to Cala del Faro, a secluded cove 2 NM east, and the sand-dune beaches of Artola. It's also a short cruise to the Marbella coastline and Puerto Banús (5 NM west). Estepona Marina gives you direct access to Playa del Cristo and Playa de la Rada, plus a 25 NM run to Gibraltar or a 10 NM cruise to Sotogrande. For day trips, Cabopino favours short hops to quiet coves, while Estepona suits longer explorations west.

Charter Options and Pickup

Our boats depart from Puerto Banús, but we can arrange a pickup at Cabopino or Estepona Marina for an additional fee. Contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability for your chosen vessel. Popular boats like the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or the Azimut 39 (€749/2h) can be delivered to either marina with advance notice. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) may require a longer lead time for relocation.

Crowds and Atmosphere

Cabopino is quieter and less commercialised than Puerto Banús, with a local fishing-port atmosphere. Estepona Marina is also relaxed but has more activity, especially on weekends. In peak summer (July–August), Cabopino can get busy with day-trippers, while Estepona remains slightly less crowded. If you want to avoid the Banús bustle entirely, both marinas offer a peaceful alternative.

Parking and Access

Cabopino's car park fills quickly on summer weekends, so arrive early. Estepona has a larger parking lot with more spaces, though fees apply at both. If you're staying at a hotel on the Golden Mile, a taxi to Cabopino costs around €15–20, while Estepona is €25–30. For a hassle-free start, consider parking at Puerto Banús and taking a water taxi to your chosen marina—a scenic 15-minute ride.

Best for Different Trip Types

  • Quick swim stops: Cabopino – Cala del Faro is 10 minutes from the marina.
  • Gibraltar day trip: Estepona – 25 NM from the Rock.
  • Family beaches: Estepona – Playa del Cristo is sheltered and shallow.
  • Romantic sunset cruise: Cabopino – quieter coves and fewer boats.
  • Luxury dining: Estepona – more restaurant options near the marina.

Frequently asked questions

Which marina is closer to Marbella town centre?

Cabopino Marina is closer, roughly 10 km (15 minutes) east of Marbella centre. Estepona Marina is about 20 km (25 minutes) west. If you're staying on the Golden Mile or in central Marbella, Cabopino is a quicker drive.

Can I charter a boat from Cabopino or Estepona Marina?

Yes, our boats depart from Puerto Banús but we can arrange a pickup at either Cabopino or Estepona Marina for an additional fee. Contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability and pricing for your chosen vessel.

What are the main attractions near Cabopino Marina?

Cabopino is famous for its sand-dune beach (Dunas de Artola) and the nearby Cala del Faro, a secluded cove ideal for swimming. The marina itself has a handful of tapas bars and a relaxed, low-key atmosphere.

What are the main attractions near Estepona Marina?

Estepona Marina is close to the historic old town with its flower-filled streets, plus the beaches of Playa del Cristo and Playa de la Rada. It's also a short cruise from Gibraltar and the luxury port of Sotogrande.

Which marina is less crowded in summer?

Estepona Marina is generally less crowded than Puerto Banús, especially in July and August. Cabopino is also quieter but can get busy on weekends with local boaters. Both offer a more relaxed alternative to Banús.

Which marina is better for a day trip to Gibraltar?

Estepona Marina is the better choice for a Gibraltar day trip, as it's only 25 nautical miles from the Rock. Cabopino is about 45 NM east, making Estepona a more practical departure point for that route.

Are there fuel stations at both marinas?

Yes, both Cabopino and Estepona marinas have fuel docks with diesel and petrol. They are open daily, though hours may vary in low season. We recommend checking ahead if you plan to refuel before departure.

Can I park my car at Cabopino or Estepona Marina?

Both marinas have paid parking lots. Cabopino's parking can fill quickly on summer weekends, while Estepona offers more spaces. If you're chartering with us, we can advise on the best place to park for your pickup.

Ready to book your charter? Compare our full fleet or check out our blog for more Marbella boating tips.
